title: Colors & Basic Adjectives
description: Describe things with colors and essential adjectives.
summary_pt_br: Cores e adjetivos básicos. Em inglês, o adjetivo vem ANTES do substantivo: 'a red car', nunca 'a car red'.

exercises.explanation: The six 'starter' colors — cover ~80% of everyday descriptions.
exercises.hint_pt_br: As seis cores mais úteis para começar.

exercises.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: I have a car of red
exercises.explanation: English word order: article → adjective → noun. 'A red car' — never 'a car red' (that's Portuguese order).
exercises.hint_pt_br: Em inglês o adjetivo vem ANTES do substantivo. 'Carro vermelho' → 'red car'.

exercises.explanation: Six adjective pairs that teachers describe as 'the survival kit' — you can describe almost anything with these.
exercises.hint_pt_br: O 'kit de sobrevivência' dos adjetivos — você descreve quase tudo com esses.

exercises.question: Complete: 'This coffee is very ___' (opposite of cold).
exercises.explanation: HOT = quente. Note: for spicy food, English uses 'spicy' or 'hot'. For temperature, it's 'hot'.
exercises.hint_pt_br: 'Hot' = quente (temperatura). Também usado para comida picante.

exercises.question: Adjectives in English change for plurals — true or false?
exercises.explanation: FALSE. English adjectives never change: 'a red car' → 'two red cars' (not 'reds cars'). Only the noun gets pluralized.
exercises.hint_pt_br: Adjetivos em inglês NUNCA mudam. 'Red car' → 'red cars'. Só o substantivo flexiona.

exercises.question: Complete: 'My house is old, but my car is ___.'
exercises.explanation: OLD ↔ NEW. A very common opposite pair.

exercises.question: Two or more adjectives together — which order?
exercises.explanation: English has a preferred adjective order: opinion/size come BEFORE origin/nationality. 'A big French car' sounds right; 'a French big car' sounds foreign.
exercises.hint_pt_br: Ordem preferida: tamanho/opinião antes de origem/nacionalidade. 'Big French car', não 'French big car'.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: What color is the sky today?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: The sky is blue and bright.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: The grass is green and soft.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: My shirt is white and clean.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: My shoes are black and old.

scenes.title: Listening story — Colors & Basic Adjectives
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça a situação com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que você entendeu — resumo, detalhes, seus pensamentos. O professor vai ler e comentar.
scenes.prompt_en: Write in English what you heard. A short summary is fine. Mention who spoke, what they said, and what you think about the situation.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que você ouviu. Pode ser um resumo curto. Diga quem falou, o que disseram e o que você achou.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: In this story, we meet Artist and a student. Describe things with colors and essential adjectives. Listen carefully and think about what each person says.

paragraphs.speaker: Artist
paragraphs.en: What color is the sky today?

paragraphs.speaker: The student
paragraphs.en: The sky is blue and bright.

paragraphs.speaker: The student
paragraphs.en: The grass is green and soft.

paragraphs.speaker: The student
paragraphs.en: My shirt is white and clean.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: That is the end of our short scene with Artist. Think about what you heard and how the conversation felt.
